title: Deputy General Manager, Regional Compliance (ASEAN)
description:
Overview

We are seeking a senior compliance leader to design and enforce a unified governance framework across our ASEAN subsidiaries. Reporting to the Regional Head of Legal & Compliance, you will act as a strategic partner to the business, ensuring that our cross-border network expansion (IPLC, IP Transit, SD-WAN) and multi-country operations align strictly with the diverse legal, tax, and telecom licensing requirements of the region.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Regional Regulatory Strategy: Oversee the regulatory roadmap for entering and operating in ASEAN markets (including Indonesia, Malaysia, Vietnam, Thailand, and Philippines). Manage the acquisition and maintenance of necessary telecom licenses (e.g., NFP/NSP/NTC) and serve as the primary escalation point for regional regulators.

  • Cross-Border Operational Governance: Harmonize "General Affairs" and HR compliance across subsidiaries. Ensure local offices adhere to their respective labor laws (e.g., Employment Act, Omnibus Law) regarding hiring, expatriate visas, and statutory benefits.

  • Data Privacy & Sovereignty: Lead the regional data privacy strategy. Navigate complex data sovereignty laws to ensure the compliant transfer of customer data and internal records between ASEAN entities and Regional HQ.

  • Commercial Risk Management: Sit on the Joint Governance committee to review complex multi-country connectivity deals. Audit vendor and partner contracts to ensure "back-to-back" liability protection and adherence to anti-corruption (ABC) standards.

  • Internal Audit & Control: Conduct regular compliance audits of local ASEAN offices, focusing on procurement integrity, safety (WSH/EHS) standards, and financial reporting.

  • Stakeholder Management: Translate regional strategy into local compliance roadmaps and act as the bridge between local ASEAN teams and the Group Headquarters.

Job RequirementsExperience & Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Law, International Business, or a related discipline. A Master’s degree or professional compliance certification is an advantage.

  • Minimum 12 years of experience in Compliance, Legal, or Regional Operations, with at least 5 years in a leadership role within the Telecommunications or ICT industry.

  • Proven track record of managing compliance or operations across multiple ASEAN jurisdictions (experience with Indonesia, Vietnam, or Malaysia is highly valued).

Technical Skills & Knowledge:

  • Strong understanding of cross-border telecom regulations (IPLC, VPN, Internet) and the licensing frameworks within Southeast Asia.

  • Familiarity with regional data privacy laws (PDPA, GDPR principles) and data sovereignty requirements.

  • Knowledge of regional labor regulations and "General Affairs" management.

Other Requirements:

  • Business-level proficiency in English and Mandarin is required. This role necessitates frequent communication with Product and Engineering teams at our Regional Headquarters in China, as well as the review of technical contracts and legal policies in Mandarin.

  • Willingness to travel within the ASEAN region (approx. 30-40%) as business needs require.

  • Legally eligible to work in Singapore.

districts.location: D01 Cecil, Marina, People’s Park, Raffles Place
